# Community Guide (AUTO GENERATED)

Welcome to the wwebjs community! This guide will help you get involved and make the most of our community resources.

## Community Channels

### Discord Server

Join our Discord server for:

- Real-time help and support
- Feature discussions
- Community events
- Direct interaction with maintainers

[Join Discord →](https://discord.gg/wwebjs)

### GitHub Discussions

Use GitHub Discussions for:

- Long-form technical discussions
- Feature requests and proposals
- Sharing projects and use cases
- Q&A with the community

[Visit Discussions →](https://github.com/wwebjs/wwebjs/discussions)

## How to Get Help

1. **Check the Documentation**: Start with our [docs](/documentation)
2. **Search Existing Issues**: Your question might already be answered
3. **Ask in Discord**: Get quick help from the community
4. **Create an Issue**: For bugs or feature requests

## Contributing

We welcome contributions! Here's how you can help:

### Code Contributions

- Fix bugs or implement features
- Improve documentation
- Add examples and tutorials
- Review pull requests

See our [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/wwebjs/wwebjs/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.

### Non-Code Contributions

- Report bugs and issues
- Suggest new features
- Help others in Discord
- Share your projects
- Improve documentation

## Community Guidelines

Please read and follow our [Code of Conduct](/about/code-of-conduct) to ensure a welcoming and inclusive community for everyone.

## Recognition

We appreciate all contributors! Contributors are recognized:

- In our GitHub repository
- On our website
- In release notes
- Through community highlights

## Events

Stay tuned for:

- Community calls
- Hackathons
- AMA sessions
- Feature showcases

Follow our Discord and GitHub for announcements!
